nodes.label: ReplacingMergeTree at-least-once deduplication rationale
nodes.rationale: ReplacingMergeTree(ingest_ts) deduplicates rows sharing the same ORDER BY key by keeping the row with the highest ingest_ts (version column). Kafka at-least-once delivery can redeliver the same event_id; using ingest_ts as the version ensures the most recently ingested copy wins after background merges. Deduplication is lazy (applied during background merges), so FINAL or GROUP BY max(ingest_ts) is needed for exact analytical counts.

nodes.label: ClickHouse three-object Kafka ingestion pipeline rationale
nodes.rationale: The canonical ClickHouse Kafka ingestion pattern uses three objects: a Kafka Engine virtual table (events_kafka) that deserializes messages without storing data, a Materialized View (events_mv) that applies column projections and type casts, and a ReplacingMergeTree (events_local) for durable storage. ClickHouse commits Kafka offsets only after the MV INSERT flushes to disk, giving at-least-once semantics; duplicates are handled by ReplacingMergeTree(ingest_ts). A separate dead-letter MV (kafka_errors_mv) routes malformed messages to kafka_errors without crashing the consumer.
nodes.norm_label: clickhouse three-object kafka ingestion pipeline rationale

nodes.label: Flink DedupFunction RocksDB state design rationale
nodes.rationale: MapState<String,Long> is used instead of ValueState<Boolean> so the event_id key is explicit in stored state and inspectable via RocksDB/savepoint tools. TTL UpdateType.OnCreateAndWrite ensures the dedup window is measured from initial ingestion; reads do not reset the clock, preventing slow consumers from indefinitely holding event_ids in state. RocksDB compaction filter removes expired entries during compaction rather than accumulating them unboundedly.
nodes.norm_label: flink dedupfunction rocksdb state design rationale

nodes.label: ValidateAndClean stateless design rationale
nodes.rationale: Keeping ETL concerns (validate + clean) in a single stateless operator minimises state and lets bad records be routed to the DLQ before consuming keyed-state slots in the downstream DedupFunction. Being stateless allows the operator to scale independently and restart instantly without a savepoint.
